name: AgentDojo
why_it_matters: Primary prompt-injection benchmark with utility/security oracles.

name: MCPTox
why_it_matters: Tool-description poisoning directly tests MCP metadata influence.

name: tau2/tau3-bench
why_it_matters: Best current local utility substrate for policy-following tool agents.

name: Skill-Inject
why_it_matters: Skill-file prompt injection is directly aligned with IntentCap's Skill story.

name: MCPSecBench
why_it_matters: Systematic MCP security playground covers clients, servers, and attacks.

name: InjecAgent
why_it_matters: Broad indirect prompt-injection corpus over user and attacker tools.

name: MCP-Bench
why_it_matters: Benign MCP utility benchmark can measure tool-exposure minimization.

name: ToolSandbox
why_it_matters: Stateful conversational tool use is useful for denial-recovery experiments.

name: HarmfulSkillBench
why_it_matters: Harmful Skills benchmark stresses refusal and skill-mediated authority.

name: WorkBench
why_it_matters: Workplace tools include email and scheduling sinks with state changes.

name: TheAgentCompany
why_it_matters: Consequential simulated company tasks stress multi-app authority.

name: SWE-bench
why_it_matters: Coding-agent tasks can test repo/shell authority after core security runs.

name: OASB Skills Security Benchmark
why_it_matters: Skill-scanner labels are useful for static-scan baselines, not runtime utility.

name: WebArena/WorkArena/BrowserGym
why_it_matters: Browser workflows stress sink selection, web context, and task utility.

name: Agent Security Bench
why_it_matters: Broad attack/defense suite covers prompt injection and memory poisoning.

name: OSWorld
why_it_matters: Desktop/file workflows are relevant to local authority but setup is heavy.

name: GAIA
why_it_matters: General assistant tasks stress web/file provenance but not protected actions.

name: BrowseComp
why_it_matters: Browsing tasks can exercise context provenance but lack side-effect actions.

name: API-Bank
why_it_matters: Runnable API dialogs can supply old but simple tool-use baselines.

name: Mind2Web/Online-Mind2Web
why_it_matters: Large web-task corpus has real web context but weak authority labels.

name: BFCL
why_it_matters: Function-call accuracy is a useful tool-schema baseline, not a security test.

name: ToolEmu
why_it_matters: LM-emulated tool risks may help generate adversarial refinement tests.

name: AgentBench
why_it_matters: Broad multi-environment agent benchmark, but less aligned with context authority.

name: WebVoyager
why_it_matters: Live multimodal web tasks are useful later but oracle cost is high.

name: HarmActionsEval
why_it_matters: Action-level harmful-tool benchmark matches gateway deny semantics.

name: AgentHarm
why_it_matters: Misuse tasks are relevant but require a safety protocol before use.

name: Agent-SafetyBench
why_it_matters: Agent safety environments can supply deny-only stress tests.

name: R-Judge
why_it_matters: Risk-awareness records may help classify denial explanations.
